title: Add Manual Trade Monitoring with Entry Point Tracking

requirements.summary: Create Trade Monitoring view with manual trade input form for asset symbol, leverage, position direction, and entry price
acceptanceCriteria: New tab/view added to main navigation for Trade Monitoring
Form accepts asset symbol with validation against Binance Futures USDT pairs
Leverage selector supports range from 1x to 125x
Direction toggle for Long/Short positions
Entry price input field with numeric validation
Form validation prevents submission with incomplete data

file_operations.description: Create main Trade Monitoring component that displays a form for manual trade input including asset symbol autocomplete (validated against Binance Futures USDT pairs fetched via existing hooks), leverage slider/selector (1x-125x), Long/Short direction toggle buttons, and entry price numeric input field. Include form validation to prevent submission with incomplete data. Display the list of saved monitored trades below the form with summary cards.

Create reusable form component for trade input with controlled inputs for symbol (with Binance USDT pair validation), leverage (1-125 range), direction (Long/Short toggle), and entry price (numeric with decimal support). Include client-side validation and disabled submit button when form is incomplete. Emit onSubmit event with validated trade data.

Add a new tab entry for 'Trade Monitoring' to the existing tabs array in the routing configuration. Register the lazy-loaded TradeMonitoring component with the tab route. Ensure the tab appears in the main navigation alongside existing tabs (market, radar, recommendations, etc.).

requirements.summary: Display real-time monitoring panel showing current price, unrealized PnL percentage, and calculated liquidation price
acceptanceCriteria: Panel displays current market price fetched from Binance
PnL calculation is accurate for both Long and Short positions
Liquidation price calculated correctly based on leverage (formula: Long liquidation = entry * (1 - 1/leverage), Short liquidation = entry * (1 + 1/leverage))
Real-time price updates every 3-5 seconds
Visual indicators (colors) change based on profit/loss status

file_operations.description: Create monitoring panel component that displays a selected trade's details including current market price (fetched via existing Binance hooks), unrealized PnL percentage calculated from entry price and current price (direction-aware: Long PnL = ((current - entry) / entry) * 100 * leverage, Short PnL = ((entry - current) / entry) * 100 * leverage), and liquidation price (Long: entry * (1 - 1/leverage), Short: entry * (1 + 1/leverage)). Apply color-coded visual indicators (green for profit, red for loss) using neon theme styling. Use React Query with refetchInterval for real-time updates every 3-5 seconds.

Create React hook that manages a single monitored trade's real-time data. Accepts trade details (symbol, entry, leverage, direction) and uses existing Binance data fetching hooks (from useQueries.ts pattern) to get current price. Computes PnL percentage and liquidation price using the formulas from REQ-2 acceptance criteria. Returns computed values with loading/error states. Configure refetch interval for real-time updates.

requirements.summary: Generate and display three suggested take-profit zones (TP1, TP2, TP3) with percentage gains, adjusted for position direction
acceptanceCriteria: Display 3 distinct take-profit zones (TP1, TP2, TP3)
TP calculations respect position direction (above entry for Long, below for Short)
Show both absolute price and percentage gain for each TP zone
TP zones adjust based on asset volatility using ATR or recent price movement data
Visual markers indicate current price position relative to TP zones

file_operations.description: Create component that displays three take-profit zones (TP1, TP2, TP3) for a monitored trade. Calculate TP prices based on direction: Long TPs are above entry (TP1 at 1-2%, TP2 at 3-5%, TP3 at 5-10%), Short TPs are below entry (same percentage ranges but inverted). Adjust percentage ranges based on asset volatility (use ATR data if available from enriched market data, otherwise use recent price movement). Display each TP with absolute price and percentage gain. Include visual progress markers showing current price position relative to each TP zone using neon-themed styling.

Create utility module for computing take-profit zones. Export function that accepts entry price, direction, and volatility metric (ATR or fallback to fixed percentages). For Long positions: calculate TP1 = entry * (1 + basePercentage1 * volatilityAdjustment), TP2 = entry * (1 + basePercentage2 * volatilityAdjustment), TP3 = entry * (1 + basePercentage3 * volatilityAdjustment). For Short positions: use subtraction instead of addition. Base percentages: TP1=1.5%, TP2=4%, TP3=7.5%. Volatility adjustment: multiply by (1 + ATR_based_factor) for high volatility assets. Return array of TP objects with price and percentage gain.

requirements.summary: Calculate and display recommended stop-loss price with risk-reward ratio, adjusted for direction and volatility
acceptanceCriteria: Stop-loss recommendation displayed with absolute price and percentage distance from entry
Direction-aware calculation (below entry for Long, above for Short)
Base stop-loss at 1-2% adjusted for high/low volatility assets
Consider nearby support/resistance zones from technical analysis when available
Show risk-reward ratio (potential gain to TP zones vs stop-loss risk)

file_operations.description: Create component that displays recommended stop-loss for a monitored trade. Calculate stop-loss price direction-aware: Long SL is below entry at 1-2% (entry * (1 - basePercentage * volatilityFactor)), Short SL is above entry (entry * (1 + basePercentage * volatilityFactor)). Use volatility data (ATR) to adjust percentage. If technical analysis support/resistance zones are available from enriched market data, adjust SL to align with nearest zone. Display SL with absolute price, percentage distance, and risk-reward ratio (distance to TP1/TP2/TP3 vs distance to SL). Apply neon-themed styling with red accent for stop-loss indicator.

Create utility module for computing stop-loss recommendations. Export function that accepts entry price, direction, volatility metric (ATR), and optional support/resistance zones from technical analysis. Base stop-loss percentage: 1.5% for low volatility, 2.5% for high volatility. For Long: SL = entry * (1 - percentage), for Short: SL = entry * (1 + percentage). If support/resistance zones are provided and within 0.5% of calculated SL, snap SL to that zone. Also compute risk-reward ratios comparing distance to each TP zone vs distance to SL. Return object with SL price, percentage, and RR ratios.

requirements.summary: Enable saving and tracking multiple monitored trades with persistent storage and summary cards
acceptanceCriteria: Users can save multiple trade setups without limit
Trades persist in localStorage or IndexedDB across sessions
List view shows all active monitored trades with summary information
Each trade card displays: symbol, long/short badge, entry price, current PnL, elapsed time
Users can select a trade to view full monitoring details
Option to close/remove completed trades from monitoring list

file_operations.description: Create React hook for managing the list of monitored trades with IndexedDB persistence (consistent with learningEngine pattern). Provide methods to add new trade, remove trade by ID, get all trades, and get single trade by ID. Store trade objects containing id (timestamp-based), symbol, leverage, direction, entryPrice, and entryTimestamp. Use React Query to manage state and enable automatic refetching of trade list when trades are added/removed. Handle IndexedDB initialization and error cases gracefully.

Create summary card component for a single monitored trade in the list view. Display symbol, direction badge (Long in green/Short in red neon styling), entry price, current PnL percentage (fetched via useMonitoredTrade hook), and elapsed time since entry (formatted as '2h 35m' or '1d 5h'). Make card clickable to select the trade for detailed monitoring panel view. Include a small close/remove button (X icon) to delete the trade from monitoring list. Apply neon-themed card styling with border colors based on current PnL status.

Create utility module for IndexedDB operations for monitored trades storage. Define MonitoredTrade type with id, symbol, leverage, direction, entryPrice, entryTimestamp fields. Export functions: initTradeDB (create/open IndexedDB with trades object store), addTrade (insert new trade with generated ID), removeTrade (delete by ID), getAllTrades (fetch all active trades), and getTradeById (fetch single trade). Handle database upgrade, migration, and error scenarios. Follow the pattern established in learningEngine.ts for consistency.

Integrate trade list and selection state management. Use useMonitoredTrades hook to load saved trades and display them as a grid of MonitoredTradeCard components. Manage selected trade state (null when no trade selected, or trade ID when a card is clicked). When a trade is selected, render the TradeMonitoringPanel with full details (current price, PnL, liquidation, TPs, SL). Wire the TradeInputForm onSubmit to call addTrade from the hook. Ensure new trades appear in the list immediately after submission.

requirements.summary: Integrate with existing Binance market data hooks for real-time price updates using frontend-only pattern
acceptanceCriteria: Reuses existing Binance Futures USD-M data fetching logic
No backend HTTP calls required (frontend-only implementation)
Price updates leverage React Query caching and refetch policies
Error handling for Binance API blocks/restrictions
Graceful degradation when market data is unavailable

file_operations.description: Ensure the hook reuses existing Binance data fetching patterns from useQueries.ts. Import and use the same binanceFetch utilities (fetchBinanceFuturesTicker or fetchBinanceFuturesAsset) to get current price for the monitored trade's symbol. Configure React Query with appropriate refetchInterval (3-5 seconds) and staleTime settings. Handle error cases including Binance API blocks/restrictions (as categorized in binanceFetch.ts) by showing graceful error messages. If market data is unavailable, display last known price with a stale indicator and disable real-time PnL updates.

If not already present, ensure there is an exported function for fetching a single asset's current price by symbol (e.g., fetchBinanceFuturesAsset) that can be used by trade monitoring. This function should accept a symbol parameter and return the current price data with the same error handling and classification as existing fetch functions. Reuse the existing error categorization for API blocks and restrictions.
